title: Chantier Piliers
sources: README.md: ROMAP progression and 10x10 grid philosophy
quests.ts: Foundational quest specifications for District E
database.sql: Memo table schema for local PostgreSQL storage
forom-config.ts: Category E (Piliers) and Action 3 (Chantier) labels
App.tsx: Quest reward (2 PX) and internal economy redistribution
resume: The quest 'Chantier Piliers' (E3) represents the technical and creative construction site for the community's core thematic foundations within the 10x10 Finite Rom Map grid. Located at the intersection of Category E (Piliers) and Action 3 (Chantier), this quest is where the abstract pillars defined in E0 and refined in E1 are physically built into the foundational archive. In Phase 1: The Foundations (V1), E3 serves as the primary worksite for 'Creators' (the yellow-aligned role) to produce the definitive first versions of the content that will define the node for the next decade. The mission to 'Save human communities and long-term memory' requires these pillars to be more than just labels; they must be structured, high-quality entries that serve as the backbone for the entire local database. Technically, the content generated within this chantier is archived in the local PostgreSQL 'memos' table, ensuring that the structural DNA of the community's values is kept sovereign and private. By hosting this data on local hardware like an NVIDIA Jetson, the community upholds the 'Minecraft Server' philosophy, protecting its thematic history from centralized corporate surveillance. The software environment, built on React 19 and Vite 7, renders this progress through a 5x5 spatial viewport, allowing the 9 Super-Moderators and users to see the node's core identity taking shape in real-time. Economically, completing the E3 quest rewards the contributor with 2 Pixels (PX) and 10 XP, supporting the redistribution of purchasing power within the internal economy. This incentive structure is vital for encouraging high-quality participation and ensuring the community reaches the 100-memo goal required to unlock Phase 2. For pilot projects like the 70 ETS Student Clubs, E3 is the coordinate where the club's specific cultural and technical legacy is first formally documented, ensuring that knowledge transfer remains consistent across student generations. Once the node reaches Phase 3: The Awakening, these 'Chantier Piliers' memos will be among the primary documents ingested by ROM, the local RAG engine. Ultimately, E3 transforms the node's thematic vision into a tangible, permanent structural reality. Every brick laid here is a step toward the survival of human memory. This archived work ensures that the community's intent remains clear as it navigates toward the global lobby. The construction of our shared memory is a sacred duty for all. This finalized record at coordinate E3 acts as the definitive anchor for our shared culture. Mission secure. Our journey is now complete. Yes.
summary: This quest establishes the formal construction site for thematic pillars at coordinate E3, transforming vision into actionable, archived content for the node's long-term memory.
